Planning for a Resilient Future

Understanding the complexity between built and natural systems in the face of climate change and extreme weather events.

Explore Our Research

About iAdapt

With ever-increasing worldwide risks from climate change, sea-level rise, and extreme weather events will greatly threaten the built and natural environment across the globe. iAdapt serves as an international knowledge-to-action exchange hub.

Our Core Objectives

1. Advance AI-enabled Urban Planning

Addressing Urban Planning AI related social and ethical issues, and initiating the development of Urban Planning AI.

2. Optimize Climate Adaptation Planning

Evaluating adaptation strategies in response to climate change and examining the impacts on coastal communities.

3. Characterize Urban Transportation

Assessing the impacts of autonomous public transportation systems, enhancing microtransit services, and examining interactions between shared mobility and spatial structure.

4. Enable Knowledge-to-Action Exchange

Building a bridge between the University of Florida and practice communities to cultivate professional adaptation planners.

Featured Research

Our recent focus areas—Urban Planning AI, Transportation Planning, and Adaptation Planning from Climate Change.

Urban Planning AI

What it is: Urban Planning AI applies artificial intelligence, machine learning, and spatial analytics to help planners interpret complex urban data, model future scenarios, and support human–AI co-creation in planning decisions.

How we do it: We develop urban analytics frameworks, apply machine learning to urban phenomena such as heat islands and post-pandemic mobility recovery, and study the social and ethical dimensions of AI-enabled planning.

View publications →

Transportation Planning

What it is: Transportation planning examines how people and goods move through cities—covering network modeling, microtransit, micromobility, autonomous vehicles, and the environmental impacts of transportation systems.

How we do it: We use activity-based travel demand models, network vulnerability analysis, on-road measurement, and implementation case studies—from Florida microtransit inventories to AV-based microtransit in Lake Nona.

View publications →

Adaptation Planning from Climate Change

What it is: Adaptation planning prepares communities for sea-level rise, flooding, hurricanes, and other climate hazards—protecting coastal populations, infrastructure, livelihoods, and the built environment.

How we do it: We build parameterized climate projection models, agent-based flood adaptation models, and policy evaluation frameworks—partnering with local governments, residents, and insurers to translate research into action.

View publications →
